inputs.label: Cutting Speed (Vc)
inputs.businessContext: ISO 3685:1993 - Tool-life testing with single-point turning tools. Cutting speed is the relative velocity between the tool and workpiece, affecting tool life and surface finish.

inputs.label: Spindle Speed (n)
inputs.businessContext: Calculated from cutting speed and workpiece diameter. Standard machining practice per ISO 3002-1.

inputs.label: Number of Teeth (z)
inputs.businessContext: Number of cutting edges on the tool. Common for milling cutters; affects feed per tooth.

inputs.label: Feed per Tooth (fz)
inputs.businessContext: ISO 3002-1:1982 - Basic quantities in cutting and grinding. Determines chip load and surface finish.

inputs.label: Material Type
inputs.businessContext: Material properties affect recommended cutting parameters. Based on Machining Data Handbook (Metcut) and ISO 513.

inputs.label: Operation Type
inputs.businessContext: Different operations have distinct feed rate formulas. Standard machining practice.

rules: If operationType == 'milling', then numberOfTeeth must be >= 1.
If operationType == 'turning', then numberOfTeeth must be 1.

thresholds.feedRateHigh: If feedRate > 5000 mm/min, warning: 'Feed rate too high; risk of tool breakage.'
thresholds.feedRateLow: If feedRate < 10 mm/min, warning: 'Feed rate too low; inefficient machining.'

hiddenLossDrivers: If feedRate > 5000: 'Excessive feed rate may cause tool wear and poor surface finish.'
If feedRate < 10: 'Low feed rate reduces productivity.'
suggestedActions: Adjust feed per tooth based on material and tool manufacturer recommendations.
Consider using higher spindle speed if feed rate is too low.
Verify tool condition if feed rate exceeds limits.

premiumFeatures: Detailed report with tool life estimation
